title: Ornithogalum perparvum
taxonomy: Ornithogalum perparvum belongs to the Asparagaceae family, a group of flowering plants commonly known as the asparagus or lily family. It is a genus of plants within the subfamily Scilloideae, which also includes the popular spring-flowering Hyacinthus and Scilla species. Ornithogalum perparvum is specifically classified in the subgenus Brunsoniae, which contains several species of Ornithogalum native to southern Africa. The species itself is further classified as Ornithogalum perparvum, a species endemic to the mountainous regions of southern Africa. Further taxonomic classification may include the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta.

intro: Ornithogalum perparvum, commonly known as the 'Star of Persia', is a bulbous perennial plant that belongs to the Asparagaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits that include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Ornithogalum perparvum include saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
